Trends to Watch in 2024 · Green Chemistry and Sustainability · Circular Economy Integration · Advanced Materials and Nanotechnology · Digitalization ...See more

FULL CONTENT:

# Top 2024 Trends for Chemical Engineers

Posted January 3rd, 2024

![Top 2024 Trends for Chemical Engineers | CPS, Inc](https://www.cps4jobs.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/cps_Trends1.jpg)

As we launch 2024, [chemical engineering](https://www.cps4jobs.com/2023/04/26/how-to-build-a-career-in-mechanical-engineering/), like many technical fields, is transforming. From sustainable practices to cutting-edge technologies, the discipline is evolving quickly to address global challenges and capitalize on emerging opportunities. This blog will delve into the top trends shaping the landscape for chemical engineers in 2024 and beyond.

## **Trends to Watch in 2024**

### **Green Chemistry and Sustainability**

The overarching trend guiding the future of [chemical engineering](https://www.cps4jobs.com/2023/02/15/most-researched-topics-for-the-chemical-engineering-industry/) is the increasing emphasis on green chemistry and sustainable practices. With climate change and environmental concerns taking center stage, chemical engineers must develop processes and materials to minimize their environmental impact. This movement involves the integration of renewable resources, energy-efficient technologies, and waste-reduction strategies into the core of chemical engineering practices.

### **Circular Economy Integration**

The shift towards a [circular economy](https://www.ellenmacarthurfoundation.org/topics/circular-economy-introduction/overview) is gaining momentum, and chemical engineers are at the forefront of this transition. Designing processes prioritizing recycling, reusing, and minimizing waste is crucial for a sustainable future. From creating eco-friendly packaging materials to developing closed-loop systems for industrial processes, chemical engineers will play a pivotal role in driving the circular economy.

### **Advanced Materials and Nanotechnology**

Breakthroughs in materials science and [nanotechnology](https://www.forbes.com/sites/chuckbrooks/2022/05/31/3-key-areas-where-nanotechnology-is-impacting-our-future/?sh=4d89b9e06741) continue to open new avenues for chemical engineers. In 2024, we can expect an increasing focus on designing advanced materials with enhanced properties for various applications, from medical devices to electronics. Nanotechnology will offer novel solutions, enabling precise control at the molecular level and the development of innovative materials with unprecedented properties.

### **Digitalization and Industry 4.0**

Integrating digital technologies into industrial processes, often called [Industry 4.0](https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/mckinsey-explainers/what-are-industry-4-0-the-fourth-industrial-revolution-and-4ir), is transforming the chemical engineering landscape. Automation, artificial intelligence, and data analytics are optimizing manufacturing processes, improving efficiency, and reducing costs. Chemical engineers must adapt to this digital era, utilizing smart sensors, machine learning algorithms, and real-time data analytics to enhance decision-making and streamline operations.

### **Bioprocessing and Biotechnology**

With the growing demand for sustainable and bio-based products, chemical engineers focus on bioprocessing and biotechnology. Integrating living organisms into manufacturing processes from biofuels to bio-based materials offers a green alternative to traditional methods. Harnessing the power of biotechnology allows for developing environmentally friendly solutions with reduced reliance on fossil fuels.

### **Hydrogen Economy**

The hydrogen economy is gaining prominence as the world seeks cleaner alternatives to traditional energy sources. Chemical engineers are instrumental in developing hydrogen production, storage, and utilization technologies. From green hydrogen production methods to hydrogen fuel cells for transportation, chemical engineers are pivotal in advancing the use of hydrogen as a clean and sustainable energy carrier.

### **Resilience in Supply Chain Management**

The disruptions caused by global events, such as the COVID-19 pandemic, have highlighted the need for resilient supply chains. Chemical engineers will focus on designing robust supply chain systems that withstand unforeseen challenges. This process includes the optimization of logistics, the integration of digital tools for real-time monitoring, and the development of agile manufacturing processes.

### **The Future of Chemical Engineering**

The field of chemical engineering is on the cusp of a transformative period, driven by a commitment to sustainability, technological innovation, and adaptability. Chemical engineers in 2024 will navigate a complex landscape, addressing global challenges and contributing to a more sustainable and technologically advanced future. As they embrace these trends, they will play a crucial role in shaping industries that are efficient, profitable, environmentally conscious, and socially responsible. CPS helps these professionals find meaningful work in the face of substantial industry change. [Our recruitment services](https://www.cps4jobs.com/recruitment-services/) help place chemical engineers in the best jobs for top companies. Contact us to find out how we can help.

--- SOURCE 3: 2025 Chemical Industry Outlook ---
URL: https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html

SUMMARY:
The chemical industry has made moderate progress in 2024, increasing year-over-year production above 2023 levels, and it is estimated that production levels will continue to rise as the destocking cycle wanes and demand rises across most products.1 However, to further support revenue growth, chemical companies have announced cost-reduction plans and begun to increase margins, while continuing to invest in decarbonization and innovation.2 In 2025, we expect the industry to continue its recovery, adjusting to new market drivers while balancing short- and long-term goals.

2025 Chemical Industry Outlook
==============================

In 2025, the chemical industry is expected to focus on innovation, sustainability, and resiliency to drive efficiency and growth
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Article

21-min read

•

04 November 2024

Deloitte Research Center for Energy & Industrials

![Image 4](https://www.deloitte.com/content/dam/insights/icons/svg/icon-alert.svg)

The chemical industry has made moderate progress in 2024, increasing year-over-year production above 2023 levels, and it is estimated that production levels will continue to rise as the destocking cycle wanes and demand rises across most products.[1](https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html#endnote-1) However, to further support revenue growth, chemical companies have announced cost-reduction plans and begun to increase margins, while continuing to invest in decarbonization and innovation.[2](https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html#endnote-2) In 2025, we expect the industry to continue its recovery, adjusting to new market drivers while balancing short- and long-term goals.

![Image 5](https://www.deloitte.com/content/dam/insights/icons/svg/icon-alert.svg)

![Image 6](https://www.deloitte.com/content/dam/insights/icons/svg/icon-alert.svg)

Since the COVID-19 pandemic began in 2020, the chemical industry has had to navigate turbulent market conditions. The pandemic led to weak demand, reduced production, and lower revenues in 2020. This was followed by a strong rebound in chemical production and revenues from 2021 to 2022, as demand rose, and fear of supply chain issues led to higher levels of inventory stocking. But by late 2022, supply chain issues began to wane, key end markets began destocking, and demand for chemicals declined. By the end of 2023, revenues decreased 8% year on year, operating margins fell to their lowest level since the Great Recession (2007 to 2009), and returns on capital dropped back to pre-pandemic levels.[3](https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html#endnote-3) These dynamics highlighted a need for greater resilience spurring cost reduction programs. While these cost-reduction programs are still rolling out, margins began to rise in the first half of 2024 (figure 1).[4](https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html#endnote-4)

![Image 7](https://www.deloitte.com/content/dam/insights/icons/svg/icon-alert.svg)

Looking ahead, moderate growth is expected to continue in the chemical industry in 2025. The American Chemistry Council (ACC) projects global chemical production to rise by 3.4% in 2024 and 3.5% in 2025, after increasing just 0.3% in 2023.[5](https://www.deloitte.com/us/en/insights/industry/chemicals-and-specialty-materials/chemical-industry-outlook.html#endnote-5) However, even with chemical production improving and margins reverting to average levels, the industry still faces challenges and uncertainty. In the next year, chemical companies will navigate many of the same challenges as other industries: evolving macroeconomic conditions, shifts in policy and regulations across regions, changing customer preferences, and advances in technology. To help improve their positions in the face of these uncertainties, chemical companies could consider adopting strategies that help them weather uncertainty while positioning themselves competitively in the low-carbon, high-tech future.

To plan for the future, companies should consider developing an understanding of where they stand in the current scenario. This can provide them with a foundation for examining the emerging trends that may shape the industry’s trajectory in the coming years. Our 2025 Chemical Industry Outlook explores some of these trends and highlights the signposts leaders should consider while developing strategies.

# Chemical Engineering in 2025 – The Future of Innovation

![The Future of Chemical Engineering In 2025 - The ChemEng Student](https://www.chemengstudent.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/Chemical-Engineering-2025.png) 

Chemical Engineering in 2025 – The Future of Innovation

There are not many fields in the world which can boast to being near perfect, but chemical engineering comes close. Chemical engineering combines principles of chemistry, physics, and **[maths](https://www.chemengstudent.com/courses/engineering-mathematics/)** to solve real world problems. Sounds good right? Read on because there is even better news – jobs! Chemical engineers have the option of working in a variety of industries such as pharmaceuticals, energy, food and beverages, and environmental sustainability. The list is endless. Now who said they wanted to be a lawyer again?

A **[chemical engineering degree](https://www.thecompleteuniversityguide.co.uk/subject-guide/chemical-engineering)** will of course be required. The degree will include generic chemical engineering modules such as: thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, reaction kinetic and **[process design](https://www.chemengstudent.com/courses/process-design/)**. Alongside these modules, students will be required to carry out laboratory experiments, work on project and complete internships to gain hands-on experience that will aid with daily job requirements.

### Future Job Opportunities

Mining is expected to create 112,000 additional jobs in Australia alone by…yeah you guessed it…2025. Sure, it’s not a chemical engineering job, but for the people left who don’t want to work in mining expect to see strong demand for chemical engineers moving forward. With green processes being the driving force of the future there are already copious inventions where chemical engineers have contributed to **[greener processes](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1877705816004689)**.

![The Future of Chemical Engineering - The ChemEng Student](https://www.chemengstudent.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Blog2-Image-1.png)

For example: fuel cells, solar cells, meeting the demand for new materials such as tissue engineering. Demand also comes from every other industry: clean energy systems, new improved sorts of renewable energy storage, super-fast communication (quantum mechanics), how to make things much better, advanced materials (are there more stable catalysts?), gas purification, carbon dioxide removal from powerplants, waste treatment etc. Do you know anyone that complains about how their phone battery does not last long enough? Yes, huge demand for better batteries as well. This is only the start…

### Industrial Trends

Looking at current examples you may have noticed subjects such as drug design and tissue engineering of late. It’s engineers who are the people getting their hands dirty to change, improve, and maybe even save our lives. Chemical Engineers are working at the forefront of an interdisciplinary field of scientists and engineers which are so rapidly advancing that we cannot begin to know where we will be in 2025: biomaterials (replacing our tissue with things that won’t be infected or we will simply grow another identical replacement). **[Tissue engineering](https://www.nibib.nih.gov/science-education/science-topics/tissue-engineering-and-regenerative-medicine#:~:text=The%20goal%20of%20tissue%20engineering,limited%20use%20in%20human%20patients.)**. Quantum computing. Genetic engineering. Chemical engineers are the missing link, providing the stuff we cannot see, with the technology needed to reach what we dream.

Also pretend the word chemical were replaced by biotechnology, then by biochemistry, one can envisage a future where the norm is chemical engineers collaborating with a range of scientists. Finally Forms of innovation spring from the ground up. The real world. The place that is as yet undefined.

### Future Prospects

**[The future prospects](https://www.chemengstudent.com/the-future-of-chemical-engineering/)** of chemical engineering are vast, with **[jobs](https://www.chemengstudent.com/chemical-engineering-jobs/)** flourishing in almost every corner of every niche you can think of. The most prevalent job prospects for chemical engineers in 2025 can be categorised into four key areas. Here is our top selection for the most prosperous opportunities in the coming years, taking into account progression, longevity, salary, and job security…

![The Future of Chemical Engineering - The ChemEng Student](https://www.chemengstudent.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/Blog2-Image-2.png)

**Pharmaceuticals** – Optimising manufacturing is right back in focus, the operating conditions just need to simulate in the lab many more times and under different conditions.

**Energy** – **[Sustainable energy](https://www.chemengstudent.com/the-role-of-chemical-engineering-in-sustainable-development/)** is no buzzword this time, this time we seem to want to actually do, but before we invest every penny, we need to develop reliable sources of energy and efficient ways of making that energy travel.

**Food and Beverages** – More original flavours, more stable flavours (what if it would last forever? Think sweet coke for 4 hours), optimised processing.

**Environmental** – Water, rain, rivers, land, air, fumes, effluent, solid waste, you get the picture.

![](https://www.chemengstudent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/29CAB31D-1415-4D24-9DD2-1DAF3EEA95D2-e1751837176562.jpg)

enriched_points.expanded_content: The future of chemical engineering is centered on sustainability, innovation, and advanced technologies, addressing global challenges like climate change and resource scarcity.

Process intensification enhances efficiency and reduces environmental impact, integrating advanced materials and digital technologies, while promoting sustainability through greener and more efficient chemical processes.

Chemical industries are increasingly adopting innovative practices, such as digitalization and circular economy principles, reflecting a shift towards sustainability and resilience in response to market demands.
